steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the main purpose of the poem. The poem opens with "while outside the tempest is raving" and then describes the calm waters of Puerto Cabello. That "while" signals a deliberate contrast between two scenes, which should be the center of any correct answer.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'It describes the speaker’s attitude towards the day-to-day life in Puerto Cabello.': The poem says nothing about daily life. It describes water conditions and weather, not how residents live. ✗
  • Choice B — 'It contrasts the tranquility of the waters near Puerto Cabello with the roughness of waters elsewhere.': This matches the poem exactly. Outside: a tempest rages and ships are "madly driving" onto shore. Inside: waters are "warm and land-locked," moving with "easy motion," and a canoe "gently glides." ✓
  • Choice C — 'It emphasizes the severity of a storm that is expected to arrive soon in Puerto Cabello.': The storm is happening outside Puerto Cabello, not approaching it. The whole point is that Puerto Cabello is sheltered from it. ✗
  • Choice D — 'It compares the appearance of two types of boats.': The poem mentions a ship and a pirogue, but their appearances are never compared. The contrast is between water conditions, not boats. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
